name: HTS_SCHED_VIOLATIONS
description: This table denotes the schedule violation messages that were raised during schedule validation execution.

columns.Name: SCHED_VIOLATION_ID
columns.Comments: The unique ID of the violation.

columns.Name: SCHED_FULL_VALIDATION_ID
columns.Comments: The full validation this validation error is associated with.

columns.Name: VALIDATION_LOOKUP_TYPE
columns.Comments: The lookup type containing the code identifying the type of validation leading to raise the violation

columns.Name: VALIDATION_LOOKUP_CODE
columns.Comments: The lookup code (combined with the lookup type above) identifying  the validation error type.

columns.Name: CONSTRAINT_ID
columns.Comments: The ID of some object denoting the constraint that is violated.

columns.Name: CONSTRAINT_TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments:
A code indicating what kind of constraint is identified by the field
CONSTRAINT_ID.

columns.Name: CONSTRAINT_SET_ID
columns.Comments: The ID of some object acting as the constraint container.

columns.Name: SEVERITY_LOOKUP_TYPE
columns.Comments: The lookup type containing the severity codes.

columns.Name: SEVERITY_LOOKUP_CODE
columns.Comments: The constraint violation severity code.

columns.Name: PERSON_ID
columns.Comments: The identifier of the worker. It can be null when the validation violation does not concern a worker.

columns.Name: ASSIGNMENT_ID
columns.Comments: The identifier of the involved assignment when the validation violation is related to the assignment.

columns.Name: SCOPE_CODE
columns.Comments: The scope of the validation/error.

columns.Name: SCOPE_START_DATE
columns.Comments: Start date of the temporal range derived from the scope of the validation error.

columns.Name: SCOPE_END_DATE
columns.Comments: End date of the temporal range derived from the scope of the validation error.

columns.Name: SCOPE_START_REF_DATE
columns.Comments: The reference start date (accounting for day start time) of the temporal range derived from the scope of the validation error.

columns.Name: SCOPE_END_REF_DATE
columns.Comments: The reference end date (accounting for day start time) of the temporal range derived from the scope of the validation error.

columns.Name: SKILL_ID
columns.Comments: The identifier of the skill involved in the validation violation.

columns.Name: VIOLATION_MESSAGE_CODE
columns.Comments: The code identifying the validation violation message.

columns.Name: VIOLATION_MESSAGE
columns.Comments: The error message formatted using the preferences of the user running the full schedule validation.

columns.Name: MESSAGE_LANGUAGE
columns.Comments: The language used to generate the "default" violation message.

columns.Name: RESOLVED
columns.Comments: Indicates whether a schedule validation violation has been resolved.

columns.Name: INCREMENTAL
columns.Comments: Indicates whether a violation was captured by an incremental validation execution (i.e., not through the initial full validation execution).

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: EnterpriseId
